mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS held below 0.5% for an extended period signals an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows, producing a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over the following quarter. The effect is measured as a positive slope in FEDFUNDS trend against 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero rate periods are not statistically higher than the full-sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals labor market healing that boosts household income expectations and consumer confidence, driving S&P 500 gains over the subsequent month as risk appetite rises.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show 20-day forward returns not significantly different from non-triggered baseline (p > 0.05), or hit rate of positive returns falls below 55% across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) growth alongside rising PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) indicates simultaneous demand strength and income support, reinforcing consumer spending capacity and lifting equity valuations over the next month. Neither series alone is sufficient; the interaction of rising sales with rising employment is required to trigger the positive signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.3% AND PAYEMS MoM growth > 0.1%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to outperform single-series triggers in forward 20-day returns by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05), or interaction Rank IC is not higher than the max of individual series Rank ICs.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and improving corporate earnings prospects, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets price in stronger near-term fundamentals.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns conditioned on positive INDPRO trend show Rank IC below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or fail a sign test at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in HOUST (housing starts) exceeding 10% above its trailing 6-month average signals renewed construction activity and household confidence, generating a durable positive impulse to equity markets over the following quarter as housing wealth effects propagate through consumption.
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST surges are followed by 60-day forward returns that are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or occur with a hit rate below 55% over rolling 3-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime, when CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, real interest rates turn increasingly negative, which initially supports equities but eventually raises inflation-risk premia and compresses valuations, producing negative 60-day forward returns once inflation exceeds a critical pace. This mechanism only activates when FEDFUNDS is constrained near zero, distinguishing it from normal-cycle rate hikes.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show 60-day forward returns that are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) relative to non-triggered baseline, or the effect reverses sign in more than one out of three rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a labor market slack regime characterized by UNRATE above 7%, a sustained rise in PAYEMS signals gradual absorption of excess labor supply, which markets interpret as durable evidence of recovery, generating persistent positive S&P 500 returns over the following month. The same PAYEMS trend is expected to have muted predictive power once UNRATE falls below full-employment thresholds.
failure_condition: PAYEMS trend loses positive Rank IC (< 0.03) with 20-day forward returns specifically during high-slack regimes, or the effect is indistinguishable from the low-slack regime coefficient (interaction term p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO growth decelerates sharply while CPIAUCSL inflation simultaneously accelerates above trend, the resulting stagflation signal raises input cost pressure without corresponding output growth, compressing corporate margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth < -0.2% AND CPIAUCSL MoM growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: Triggered stagflation episodes fail to show significantly negative 5-day forward returns (p > 0.05) versus baseline, or hit rate of negative returns is below 55% across available triggered instances.
